description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a rectangular main structure and an attached single-story extension at the rear. The exterior walls are finished in a weathered beige plaster, with visible stonework at the base and around the windows. The roof is covered with reddish-brown ceramic tiles, featuring a prominent gable design and a small chimney. The front facade has symmetrical window placement and a central entry door, while the rear and side elevations show fewer windows and a utilitarian appearance. The house sits on a grassy plot, with some paved areas visible near the side and back. Architectural details suggest a traditional Central or Eastern European rural style, with simple, functional lines and minimal ornamentation.
description: A two-story, beige plaster house with a red-tiled gable roof, symmetrical front windows, and a rear extension, set on a grassy plot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with symmetrical windows, central entry door, and stonework at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the house, a single window, and a paved area adjacent to the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the attached single-story extension, back door, and fewer windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gable end, a single window, and the transition between the main house and the extension.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front, sparse on sides and rear
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with stone steps, minimal porch, and simple rectangular design
